query: How do LLM outputs degrade (context rot, unclear instructions, excessive tokens) and what techniques combat it?
assumptions: Findings on frontier 2025-2026 models generalize to commonly used models (supported by Chroma's 18-model sweep).
Task-accuracy and text-quality proxies meaningfully approximate 'output quality'.
Scoped to context/token-driven degradation; quantization, RLHF/sycophancy, and data contamination noted as out-of-scope gaps.
